hypothesis: If metal superhydride discovery is structured around the chemical concept of quasi-atom electron localization at interstitial sites in metal lattices, then a machine learning workflow trained on known structural prototypes using this chemical template theory will more efficiently identify thermodynamically stable and high-temperature superconducting metal superhydrides than unguided high-throughput structural searches, because constraining the search space to chemically meaningful metal lattice configurations reduces computational cost while preserving the critical structural features responsible for stability and strong electron-phonon coupling.
